commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=05484c7a41e4cf24cf358578ccfbbfdfbfe5197b)
commands.review_summary:
This PR routes repair checkpoint commits through a new explicit `must_touch`/`must_touch_files` contract helper and adds source-inspection tests for checkpoint coverage and porcelain parsing.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch blockers: source inspection shows the schema disallows `must_touch` and `must_touch_files` while the helper relies on those fields. The PR body's local proof manually injects the unsupported fields, so it does not reproduce the production artifact path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is a maintainer contract-design decision plus nontrivial parser/test repair, not a safe automated patch on the current branch.

target.title: Enforce repair contract at every checkpoint
target.body:
## Summary

Rebuild the closed #336 repair-contract idea from current `main` with the maintainer-requested shape:

```text
- start from current main
- no conflict-prompt changes from the closed branch
- centralize checkpointing behind one contract-enforcing helper
- guard all four checkpoint paths
- only fail closed when the artifact has an explicit must_touch / must_touch_files contract
- include focused bypass/parser/checkpoint coverage
```

This intentionally does **not** use `likely_files` as a hard gate. If `must_touch` / `must_touch_files` is absent, the checkpoint helper bypasses the contract so stale or incomplete `likely_files` cannot block otherwise valid repairs.

## Scope

```text
src/repair/execute-fix-artifact.ts
- replace all four commitCheckpointIfNeeded call sites with commitRepairCheckpointIfNeeded
- enforce explicit must_touch / must_touch_files before checkpoint commits
- parse git porcelain paths with per-line offset preservation
- handle rename output by using the destination path

test/repair/execute-fix-artifact-source.test.ts
- assert no unguarded checkpoint helper remains
- assert all four checkpoint phases use the guarded helper
- assert likely_files is not used as the fail-closed contract
- assert porcelain parsing uses line.slice(3) and rename handling
```

## Validation

```text
corepack pnpm run build:repair
node --test test/repair/execute-fix-artifact-source.test.ts
corepack pnpm run lint:repair
corepack pnpm run format:check
```

Result:

```text
build:repair: passed
execute-fix-artifact-source.test.ts: 9/9 passed
lint:repair: 0 warnings, 0 errors
format:check: passed
```

A full `pnpm check` was started locally and reached build/lint/test execution, but the terminal session timed out before the full suite completed. The focused commands above cover this PR's changed repair surface.

## Local repair-worker proof

I also ran `node dist/repair/execute-fix-artifact.js ... --dry-run` through a local repair-worker harness with `CODEX_BIN` set to a deterministic local fake Codex binary. The harness exercised the real executor, git checkout, Codex subprocess path, checkpoint helper, and dry-run report path. It did not push or open PRs.

### 1. Allowed checkpoint when `must_touch` is changed

Setup:

```text
fix_artifact.must_touch = ["docs/repair-contract-proof.md"]
fake worker edit = docs/repair-contract-proof.md
```

Observed result:

```text
exit: 0
checkpoint commit created: f99581f1cf test: local repair contract proof
git_status after run: clean
```

The dry-run report later became `blocked` because repository validation forced `pnpm check:changed` in the temporary clone and exceeded the short proof timeout. That happens after the checkpoint gate already accepted and committed the explicit `must_touch` edit; it is not a contract rejection.

### 2. Rejected checkpoint when only an off-contract file is changed

Setup:

```text
fix_artifact.must_touch = ["docs/repair-contract-proof.md"]
fake worker edit = docs/off-contract-proof.md
```

Observed result:

```text
exit: 1
head remained at base commit: c88270b818
working tree: ?? docs/off-contract-proof.md
```

Error:

```text
Error: repair checkpoint contract rejected initial: no must_touch file changed; must_touch=docs/repair-contract-proof.md; changed_files=docs/off-contract-proof.md
```

This demonstrates the requested worker-level boundary: an explicit contract file can checkpoint, while an unrelated edit is stopped before checkpoint commit.

## Relation to closed PRs

- Supersedes the useful parser/checkpoint idea from `Jhacarreiro/clawsweeper#2`.
- Replaces the closed #336 approach rather than reopening it.
- Excludes the conflict-prompt changes that already landed via #332.
target.author: jhacarreiro

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
